text.title: Overlay — Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the shape of an `OverlayResolution` and audit event for each resolved methodology load.

list.item: `files` — object mapping file path → `{source: enum {REPO, USER, SHIPPED}, content_id, merge_mode}`
`conflicts` — array of `{file, conflict_type, message}` (may be empty)
`version_skew` — boolean — true if any USER/REPO content_id differs from SHIPPED

antipattern.description: Resolution emitted with `conflicts.length > 0` but no message surfaced to operator.
antipattern.reason: Silent conflicts cause production drift. Operator must see and resolve before runtime.

antipattern.description: `merge_mode: APPEND` applied to `AGENTS.md` (markdown file with no `<text id>`).
antipattern.reason: Violates r3. APPEND/PREPEND require structured XML target.

antipattern.description: Overlay loads methodology marked `non-overridable: true` in shipped frontmatter (e.g. security-critical core rules).
antipattern.reason: Violates r4(c). Some methodologies are tier-gate primitives that overlaying corrupts.

list.item: Overlay may add new `tags:` entries in frontmatter without conflict (additive metadata).
Overlay may bump `version:` independently of shipped (team versioning is local).
Overlay may set `maintainers: [team-name]` to override shipped maintainer.
All resolved files have a `source` in {REPO, USER, SHIPPED}.
`version_skew: false` OR every skewed file has an explicit maintainer ack.
No `non-overridable` shipped methodology in overlay set.
